I cut out all refined sugar 6 weeks ago. I’m eating lots of fruit and salads with salmon, sardines, tuna or chicken. I’ve already lost 20lbs. I’ve also cut out all caffeine and have seen my blood pressure drop to well within the healthy range from being excessively high. I feel better than I have in years. I did contemplate cheating with jabs but I’m scared of needles and a good friend had to have surgery to have his gall bladder removed due to using the jabs. That put me off doing that!

When I was in my 20's, I dealt with several UTI and bladder infections. I saw several doctors, took numerous meds, etc. The last doctor I saw for this asked if I took baths or if I showered. I took a bath and would often soak for a long time. The doctor told me to start showering. I followed his advice and can't remember the last time I had a UTI or bladder infection. I'm now approaching 80. Maybe I was simple lucky I don't know. Maybe this helps.

Activists accused of trying to develop an American-style 'white-only' community in the Welsh countryside face a confrontation with the local council after building on their woodland site without planning permission. The Woodlander Initiative (TWI), led by veteran fascist Simon Birkett, has raised £235,000 of its £1million target towards buying land in every British county. It appears to be an attempt to create a UK version of the American Return To The Land (RTTL) movement, which has set up a white separatist enclave in Arkansas with the goal of resegregating society.

The England and Newcastle legend was diagnosed with stage four stomach cancer in January, having been admitted to hospital with abdominal problems. Keegan made his name at Liverpool in a glittering playing career. He won 63 England caps, and managed Fulham and Manchester City. Never mind, they deserve to be banned from the next world cup.

Argentina captain Lionel Messi gave a curious message to his players just moments before their World Cup final defeat by Spain, new footage has revealed. Spain beat the 2022 world champions 1-0 at the MetLife Stadium on Sunday, triumphing thanks to Ferran Torres' goal in extra time after dominating the ball in one of the most one-sided World Cup finals ever staged. Argentina, who had controversially edged past Cape Verde, Egypt and Switzerland before knocking England out in the semi-finals, were heavily criticised for their aggressive tackling and dirty tactics throughout the tournament - and their World Cup campaign ended in disgrace as they became embroiled in a mass brawl with Spain's players after the final whistle.

Argentina try to control the game by committing cynical fouls in safe areas, to stop the opposition transitioning quickly to counter attack. While Messi's arguing with the ref or standing over the free kick, the rest of the players get behind the ball and reset their defensive shape. They break up and slow down the game by surrounding the ref, complaining about every decision against them as if it's a gross injustice. Then, when one of their players gets fouled they do the same, acting outraged to make sure they get a free kick and trying to get a yellow card. Argentina keep constant pressure on the match officials, antagonising the opposition and the ref to test and unsettle them. They have skilful footballers but they are very cynical about the way they play the game and, we saw clearly at this World Cup, that they are not good sportsmen.

As an elderly person ( 80+)with rental properties I suggest that that you find a new accountant, then get a solicitor to write to both the accountant and financial adviser advising them that their services are no longer required and would they send copies of the last 6 years records. Plus I would get the rental properties valued, with interest rates going up and the owner having dementia it could be worth selling them and moving into cash, it could take a couple of years to sell as tenants have better rights, what you dont want is in say 5 years as the dementia gets worse the person dies with the tenants still there as it would be a right mess to sort out and very time-consuming especially as you are not a relative. I am selling all my rentals now and even after paying CGT I will have a substantial sum to invest at 4% +

Millie Mackintosh describes moving out of the marital home she shared with ex Hugo Taylor as 'healing' and says her next chapter is on 'her terms'

Millie Mackintosh officially moved out of the marital home she shared with her ex husband Hugo Taylor on Monday.  The former Made In Chelsea stars split in early February after seven years of marriage, during which time they welcomed daughters Sienna, six, and Aurelia, four. And opening up in her recent Instagram, Millie shared snaps from the moving process as she revealed that while divorce isn't what she had imagined for herself, she has found the moving process a 'healing' one.

Poor little creature. Glad it was rescued.

The terrified common marmoset was discovered last Friday at 11.43am on board the 302 bus, travelling between Mill Hill Broadway Station and Kensal Rise. The driver had pushed the laundry bag out of the way for a mother and her pram trying to move down the bus, only for the bag to start 'chirping and jumping around'. It was then that the driver peered behind the bag and discovered the tiny primate quivering inside the cage.
  • 88 comments
